To facilitate animation and prevent the leakage of the shape of the driving object, we disentangle shape and pose of objects in the region space. Our model can animate a variety of objects, surpassing previous methods by a large margin on existing benchmarks. We present a challenging new benchmark with high-resolution videos and show that the improvement is particularly pronounced when articulated objects are considered, reaching 96.6% user preference vs. the state of the art. 我们提出了新颖的运动表示，用于对由不同部分组成的铰接对象进行动画处理。我们的方法以完全无监督的方式识别物体部分，在驾驶视频中跟踪它们，并通过考虑它们的主轴来推断它们的运动。与之前基于关键点的工作相比，我们的方法提取有意义且一致的区域，描述位置、形状和姿势。这些区域对应于语义相关且不同的对象部分，这些部分在驾驶视频的帧中更容易被检测到。为了强制前景与背景解耦，我们使用附加的仿射变换对非对象相关的全局运动进行建模。为了方便动画并防止驾驶物体的形状泄漏，我们在区域空间中解开了物体的形状和姿势。我们的模型可以对各种对象进行动画处理，在现有基准上大大超过了以前的方法。我们提出了一个具有挑战性的高分辨率视频新基准，并表明当考虑铰接式物体时，改进尤其明显，与现有技术相比，达到了 96.6% 的用户偏好。 本文提出了一种新颖的运动表示方法，用于动画化具有不同部分的 articulated 对象，通过无监督学习识别物体部分并推断其运动。 Aliaksandr Siarohin,Oliver J. Woodford,Jian Ren,Menglei Chai,Sergey Tulyakov cs.CV

authors_text: Lumbardh Elshani,Krenare Pireva Nuçi
title: Constructing a personalized learning path using genetic algorithms approach
title_zh: 使用遗传算法方法构建个性化学习路径
abstract: A substantial disadvantage of traditional learning is that all students follow the same learning sequence, but not all of them have the same background of knowledge, the same preferences, the same learning goals, and the same needs. Traditional teaching resources, such as textbooks, in most cases pursue students to follow fixed sequences during the learning process, thus impairing their performance. Learning sequencing is an important research issue as part of the learning process because no fixed learning paths will be appropriate for all learners. For this reason, many research papers are focused on the development of mechanisms to offer personalization on learning paths, considering the learner needs, interests, behaviors, and abilities. In most cases, these researchers are totally focused on the student's preferences, ignoring the level of difficulty and the relation degree that exists between various concepts in a course. This research paper presents the possibility of constructing personalized learning paths using genetic algorithm-based model, encountering the level of difficulty and relation degree of the constituent concepts of a course. The experimental results shows that the genetic algorithm is suitable to generate optimal learning paths based on learning object difficulty level, duration, rating, and relation degree between each learning object as elementary parts of the sequence of the learning path. From these results compared to the quality of the traditional learning path, we observed that even the quality of the weakest learning path generated by our GA approach is in a favor compared to quality of the traditional learning path, with a difference of 3.59\%, while the highest solution generated in the end resulted 8.34\% in favor of our proposal compared to the traditional learning paths.
summary: A substantial disadvantage of traditional learning is that all students follow the same learning sequence, but not all of them have the same background of knowledge, the same preferences, the same learning goals, and the same needs. Traditional teaching resources, such as textbooks, in most cases pursue students to follow fixed sequences during the learning process, thus impairing their performance. Learning sequencing is an important research issue as part of the learning process because no fixed learning paths will be appropriate for all learners. For this reason, many research papers are focused on the development of mechanisms to offer personalization on learning paths, considering the learner needs, interests, behaviors, and abilities. In most cases, these researchers are totally focused on the student's preferences, ignoring the level of difficulty and the relation degree that exists between various concepts in a course. This research paper presents the possibility of constructing personalized learning paths using genetic algorithm-based model, encountering the level of difficulty and relation degree of the constituent concepts of a course. The experimental results shows that the genetic algorithm is suitable to generate optimal learning paths based on learning object difficulty level, duration, rating, and relation degree between each learning object as elementary parts of the sequence of the learning path. From these results compared to the quality of the traditional learning path, we observed that even the quality of the weakest learning path generated by our GA approach is in a favor compared to quality of the traditional learning path, with a difference of 3.59\%, while the highest solution generated in the end resulted 8.34\% in favor of our proposal compared to the traditional learning paths.
abstract_zh: 传统学习的一个重大缺点是所有学生都遵循相同的学习顺序，但并非所有人都具有相同的知识背景、相同的偏好、相同的学习目标和相同的需求。传统的教学资源，例如教科书，在大多数情况下都要求学生在学习过程中遵循固定的顺序，从而损害了他们的表现。作为学习过程的一部分，学习排序是一个重要的研究问题，因为没有固定的学习路径适合所有学习者。因此，许多研究论文都专注于开发提供个性化学习路径的机制，考虑学习者的需求、兴趣、行为和能力。在大多数情况下，这些研究人员完全关注学生的喜好，而忽略了课程的难度程度和各个概念之间存在的相关程度。该研究论文提出了使用基于遗传算法的模型构建个性化学习路径的可能性，遇到课程组成概念的难度级别和相关程度。实验结果表明，遗传算法适合根据学习对象的难度等级、持续时间、评分以及每个学习对象之间的相关度作为学习路径序列的基本部分来生成最优学习路径。从这些结果与传统学习路径的质量相比，我们观察到，即使是我们的 GA 方法生成的最弱学习路径的质量也比传统学习路径的质量有优势，差异为 3.59%，而最终生成的最高解决方案与传统学习路径相比，有 8.34% 有利于我们的建议。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于遗传算法的个性化学习路径构建方法，以解决传统学习路径固定、无法适应学生个体差异的问题。
AI.motivation: 传统学习路径固定，忽视学生知识背景、兴趣、能力差异及知识点间的关联性，导致学习效果不佳。
AI.method: 采用遗传算法模型，综合考虑学习对象的难度等级、持续时间、评分及知识点关联度，生成个性化学习路径。
AI.result: 实验表明，遗传算法生成的最差学习路径较传统方法提升3.59%，最优方案提升8.34%。
AI.conclusion: 遗传算法能有效生成优于传统固定路径的个性化学习方案，显著提升学习路径质量。

authors_text: Rohan Wadhawan,Tapan K. Gandhi
title: Landmark-Aware and Part-based Ensemble Transfer Learning Network for Facial Expression Recognition from Static images
title_zh: 用于静态图像面部表情识别的地标感知和基于部分的集成迁移学习网络
abstract: Facial Expression Recognition from static images is a challenging problem in computer vision applications. Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), the state-of-the-art method for various computer vision tasks, has had limited success in predicting expressions from faces having extreme poses, illumination, and occlusion conditions. To mitigate this issue, CNNs are often accompanied by techniques like transfer, multi-task, or ensemble learning that often provide high accuracy at the cost of increased computational complexity. In this work, we propose a Part-based Ensemble Transfer Learning network that models how humans recognize facial expressions by correlating the spatial orientation pattern of the facial features with a specific expression. It consists of 5 sub-networks, and each sub-network performs transfer learning from one of the five subsets of facial landmarks: eyebrows, eyes, nose, mouth, or jaw to expression classification. We show that our proposed ensemble network uses visual patterns emanating from facial muscles' motor movements to predict expressions and demonstrate the usefulness of transfer learning from Facial Landmark Localization to Facial Expression Recognition. We test the proposed network on the CK+, JAFFE, and SFEW datasets, and it outperforms the benchmark for CK+ and JAFFE datasets by 0.51% and 5.34%, respectively. Additionally, the proposed ensemble network consists of only 1.65M model parameters, ensuring computational efficiency during training and real-time deployment. Grad-CAM visualizations of our proposed ensemble highlight the complementary nature of its sub-networks, a key design parameter of an effective ensemble network. Lastly, cross-dataset evaluation results reveal that our proposed ensemble has a high generalization capacity, making it suitable for real-world usage.
summary: Facial Expression Recognition from static images is a challenging problem in computer vision applications. Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), the state-of-the-art method for various computer vision tasks, has had limited success in predicting expressions from faces having extreme poses, illumination, and occlusion conditions. To mitigate this issue, CNNs are often accompanied by techniques like transfer, multi-task, or ensemble learning that often provide high accuracy at the cost of increased computational complexity. In this work, we propose a Part-based Ensemble Transfer Learning network that models how humans recognize facial expressions by correlating the spatial orientation pattern of the facial features with a specific expression. It consists of 5 sub-networks, and each sub-network performs transfer learning from one of the five subsets of facial landmarks: eyebrows, eyes, nose, mouth, or jaw to expression classification. We show that our proposed ensemble network uses visual patterns emanating from facial muscles' motor movements to predict expressions and demonstrate the usefulness of transfer learning from Facial Landmark Localization to Facial Expression Recognition. We test the proposed network on the CK+, JAFFE, and SFEW datasets, and it outperforms the benchmark for CK+ and JAFFE datasets by 0.51% and 5.34%, respectively. Additionally, the proposed ensemble network consists of only 1.65M model parameters, ensuring computational efficiency during training and real-time deployment. Grad-CAM visualizations of our proposed ensemble highlight the complementary nature of its sub-networks, a key design parameter of an effective ensemble network. Lastly, cross-dataset evaluation results reveal that our proposed ensemble has a high generalization capacity, making it suitable for real-world usage.
abstract_zh: 静态图像的面部表情识别是计算机视觉应用中的一个具有挑战性的问题。卷积神经网络 (CNN) 是用于各种计算机视觉任务的最先进方法，但在预测具有极端姿势、光照和遮挡条件的面部表情方面取得的成功有限。为了缓解这个问题，CNN 通常伴随着迁移、多任务或集成学习等技术，这些技术通常以增加计算复杂性为代价提供高精度。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种基于部分的集成迁移学习网络，该网络通过将面部特征的空间方向模式与特定表情相关联来模拟人类如何识别面部表情。它由 5 个子网络组成，每个子网络执行从面部标志的五个子集之一（眉毛、眼睛、鼻子、嘴巴或下巴）到表情分类的迁移学习。我们展示了我们提出的集成网络使用面部肌肉运动运动发出的视觉模式来预测表情，并证明了从面部标志定位到面部表情识别的迁移学习的有用性。我们在 CK+、JAFFE 和 SFEW 数据集上测试了所提出的网络，它的性能分别比 CK+ 和 JAFFE 数据集的基准高出 0.51% 和 5.34%。此外，所提出的集成网络仅由 1.65M 模型参数组成，保证了训练和实时部署期间的计算效率。我们提出的集成的 Grad-CAM 可视化突出了其子网络的互补性，这是有效集成网络的关键设计参数。最后，跨数据集评估结果表明，我们提出的集成具有很高的泛化能力，使其适合现实世界的使用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于面部关键点的集成迁移学习网络，用于从静态图像中进行面部表情识别。
AI.motivation: 传统卷积神经网络在极端姿态、光照和遮挡条件下识别面部表情效果有限，且现有方法计算复杂度高。
AI.method: 设计包含5个子网络的集成架构，每个子网络通过迁移学习将面部关键点（眉毛、眼睛、鼻子、嘴巴、下颌）特征映射到表情分类任务。
AI.result: 在CK+和JAFFE数据集上分别超越基准0.51%和5.34%，参数量仅1.65M，Grad-CAM验证子网络互补性，跨数据集泛化能力优异。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过迁移学习有效利用面部肌肉运动模式，实现高精度低复杂度的表情识别，适用于实际场景部署。

Lastly, cross-dataset evaluation results reveal that our proposed ensemble has a high generalization capacity, making it suitable for real-world usage. 静态图像的面部表情识别是计算机视觉应用中的一个具有挑战性的问题。卷积神经网络 (CNN) 是用于各种计算机视觉任务的最先进方法，但在预测具有极端姿势、光照和遮挡条件的面部表情方面取得的成功有限。为了缓解这个问题，CNN 通常伴随着迁移、多任务或集成学习等技术，这些技术通常以增加计算复杂性为代价提供高精度。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种基于部分的集成迁移学习网络，该网络通过将面部特征的空间方向模式与特定表情相关联来模拟人类如何识别面部表情。它由 5 个子网络组成，每个子网络执行从面部标志的五个子集之一（眉毛、眼睛、鼻子、嘴巴或下巴）到表情分类的迁移学习。我们展示了我们提出的集成网络使用面部肌肉运动运动发出的视觉模式来预测表情，并证明了从面部标志定位到面部表情识别的迁移学习的有用性。我们在 CK+、JAFFE 和 SFEW 数据集上测试了所提出的网络，它的性能分别比 CK+ 和 JAFFE 数据集的基准高出 0.51% 和 5.34%。此外，所提出的集成网络仅由 1.65M 模型参数组成，保证了训练和实时部署期间的计算效率。我们提出的集成的 Grad-CAM 可视化突出了其子网络的互补性，这是有效集成网络的关键设计参数。最后，跨数据集评估结果表明，我们提出的集成具有很高的泛化能力，使其适合现实世界的使用。 本文提出一种基于面部关键点的集成迁移学习网络，用于从静态图像中进行面部表情识别。 Rohan Wadhawan,Tapan K. Gandhi cs.CV,cs.LG,cs.NE,eess.IV

authors_text: Humberto De las Casas,Nicholas Chambers,Hanz Richter,Kenneth Sparks
title: Real-Time Trajectory Optimization in Robot-Assisted Exercise and Rehabilitation
title_zh: 机器人辅助运动和康复中的实时轨迹优化
abstract: This work focuses on the optimization of the training trajectory orientation using a robot as an advanced exercise machine (AEM) and muscle activations as biofeedback. Muscle recruitment patterns depend on trajectory parameters of the AEMs and correlate with the efficiency of exercise. Thus, improvements to training efficiency may be achieved by optimizing these parameters. The optimal regulation of these parameters is challenging because of the complexity of the physiological dynamics from person to person as a result of the unique physical features such as musculoskeletal distribution. Furthermore, these effects can vary due to fatigue, body temperature, and other physiological factors. In this paper, a model-free optimization method using Extremum Seeking Control (ESC) as a real-time optimizer is proposed. After selecting a muscle objective, this method seeks for the optimal combination of parameters using the muscle activations as biofeedback. The muscle objective can be selected by a therapist to emphasize or de-emphasize certain muscle groups. The feasibility of this method has been proven for the automatic regulation of an ellipsoidal curve orientation, suggesting the existence of two local optimal orientations. This methodology can also be applied to other parameter regulations using a different physiological effects such as oxygen consumption and heart rate as biofeedback.
summary: This work focuses on the optimization of the training trajectory orientation using a robot as an advanced exercise machine (AEM) and muscle activations as biofeedback. Muscle recruitment patterns depend on trajectory parameters of the AEMs and correlate with the efficiency of exercise. Thus, improvements to training efficiency may be achieved by optimizing these parameters. The optimal regulation of these parameters is challenging because of the complexity of the physiological dynamics from person to person as a result of the unique physical features such as musculoskeletal distribution. Furthermore, these effects can vary due to fatigue, body temperature, and other physiological factors. In this paper, a model-free optimization method using Extremum Seeking Control (ESC) as a real-time optimizer is proposed. After selecting a muscle objective, this method seeks for the optimal combination of parameters using the muscle activations as biofeedback. The muscle objective can be selected by a therapist to emphasize or de-emphasize certain muscle groups. The feasibility of this method has been proven for the automatic regulation of an ellipsoidal curve orientation, suggesting the existence of two local optimal orientations. This methodology can also be applied to other parameter regulations using a different physiological effects such as oxygen consumption and heart rate as biofeedback.
abstract_zh: 这项工作的重点是使用机器人作为高级运动机（AEM）和肌肉激活作为生物反馈来优化训练轨迹方向。肌肉募集模式取决于 AEM 的轨迹参数，并与运动效率相关。因此，可以通过优化这些参数来提高训练效率。这些参数的最佳调节具有挑战性，因为由于肌肉骨骼分布等独特的物理特征，人与人之间的生理动力学非常复杂。此外，这些影响可能因疲劳、体温和其他生理因素而异。本文提出了一种使用极值搜索控制（ESC）作为实时优化器的无模型优化方法。选择肌肉目标后，该方法使用肌肉激活作为生物反馈来寻求参数的最佳组合。治疗师可以选择肌肉目标来强调或弱化某些肌肉群。该方法对于椭圆曲线方向自动调节的可行性已被证明，表明存在两个局部最优方向。该方法还可以应用于使用不同的生理效应（例如耗氧量和心率作为生物反馈）的其他参数调节。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于无模型极值搜索控制（ESC）的实时轨迹优化方法，通过肌肉激活作为生物反馈，优化机器人辅助训练中的轨迹参数以提高康复效率。
AI.motivation: 不同个体的生理特性（如肌肉骨骼分布、疲劳、体温等）导致训练轨迹参数优化复杂，需实时调整以提升康复效果并个性化训练。
AI.method: 采用无模型极值搜索控制（ESC）作为实时优化器，利用肌肉激活数据作为生物反馈，根据治疗师设定的肌肉目标动态调整轨迹参数。
AI.result: 验证了该方法在椭圆曲线方向自动调节中的可行性，发现存在两个局部最优方向，并可扩展至其他生理指标（如氧耗、心率）的优化。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为个性化康复训练提供了实时优化框架，通过生物反馈自适应调整参数，具有广泛的应用潜力。

authors_text: Hansoo Lee,Joonyoung Park,Uichin Lee
title: A Systematic Survey on Android API Usage for Data-Driven Analytics with Smartphones
title_zh: 针对智能手机数据驱动分析的 Android API 使用情况的系统调查
comment: This paper has been accepted by ACM Computing Surveys (CSUR), 2022
comments: This paper has been accepted by ACM Computing Surveys (CSUR), 2022
abstract: Recent industrial and academic research has focused on data-driven analytics with smartphones by collecting user interaction, context, and device systems data through Application Programming interfaces (APIs) and sensors. The Android OS provides various APIs to collect such mobile usage and sensor data for third-party developers. Usage Statistics API (US API) and Accessibility Service API (AS API) are representative Android APIs for collecting app usage data and are used for various research purposes as they can collect fine-grained interaction data (e.g., app usage history, user interaction type). Furthermore, other sensor APIs help to collect a user's context and device state data, along with AS/US APIs. This review investigates mobile usage and sensor data-driven research using AS/US APIs, by categorizing the research purposes and the data types. In this paper, the surveyed studies are classified as follows: five themes and 21 subthemes, and a four-layer hierarchical data classification structure. This allows us to identify a data usage trend and derive insight into data collection according to research purposes. Several limitations and future research directions of mobile usage and sensor data-driven analytics research are discussed, including the impact of changes in the Android API versions on research, the privacy and data quality issues, and the mitigation of reproducibility risks with standardized data typology.
summary: Recent industrial and academic research has focused on data-driven analytics with smartphones by collecting user interaction, context, and device systems data through Application Programming interfaces (APIs) and sensors. The Android OS provides various APIs to collect such mobile usage and sensor data for third-party developers. Usage Statistics API (US API) and Accessibility Service API (AS API) are representative Android APIs for collecting app usage data and are used for various research purposes as they can collect fine-grained interaction data (e.g., app usage history, user interaction type). Furthermore, other sensor APIs help to collect a user's context and device state data, along with AS/US APIs. This review investigates mobile usage and sensor data-driven research using AS/US APIs, by categorizing the research purposes and the data types. In this paper, the surveyed studies are classified as follows: five themes and 21 subthemes, and a four-layer hierarchical data classification structure. This allows us to identify a data usage trend and derive insight into data collection according to research purposes. Several limitations and future research directions of mobile usage and sensor data-driven analytics research are discussed, including the impact of changes in the Android API versions on research, the privacy and data quality issues, and the mitigation of reproducibility risks with standardized data typology.
abstract_zh: 最近的工业和学术研究重点关注智能手机的数据驱动分析，通过应用程序编程接口 (API) 和传感器收集用户交互、上下文和设备系统数据。 Android 操作系统提供了各种 API 来为第三方开发人员收集此类移动使用情况和传感器数据。使用统计 API (US API) 和辅助服务 API (AS API) 是用于收集应用程序使用数据的代表性 Android API，并且可用于各种研究目的，因为它们可以收集细粒度的交互数据（例如，应用程序使用历史记录、用户交互类型）。此外，其他传感器 API 以及 AS/US API 有助于收集用户的上下文和设备状态数据。本综述通过对研究目的和数据类型进行分类，调查了使用 AS/US API 的移动使用和传感器数据驱动的研究。本文将调查研究分类为：5个主题和21个子主题，以及四层分层数据分类结构。这使我们能够识别数据使用趋势并根据研究目的深入了解数据收集。讨论了移动使用和传感器数据驱动分析研究的一些局限性和未来的研究方向，包括 Android API 版本的变化对研究的影响、隐私和数据质量问题，以及通过标准化数据类型减轻再现性风险。

AI.tldr: 本文系统回顾了基于Android API的智能手机数据驱动分析研究，重点分析了使用统计API和无障碍服务API收集用户行为及上下文数据的方法。
AI.motivation: 随着智能手机数据采集需求的增长，需梳理现有研究对Android API的应用模式，明确数据分类框架并探讨隐私、可重复性等挑战。
AI.method: 通过分类整理5大主题21个子主题，构建四层数据分类结构，分析基于AS/US API及其他传感器API的研究案例与数据使用趋势。
AI.result: 识别出数据使用趋势，揭示了API版本变更、隐私保护及数据质量等局限性，并提出标准化数据分类以降低研究风险。
AI.conclusion: 现有研究需关注API兼容性、隐私合规性及数据标准化，未来应建立更系统的数据分类框架以提升研究可重复性。

authors_text: Andrew K. Saydjari,Douglas P. Finkbeiner
title: Equivariant Wavelets: Fast Rotation and Translation Invariant Wavelet Scattering Transforms
title_zh: 等变小波：快速旋转和平移不变小波散射变换
comment: 20 pages, 14 figures, submitted to IEEE Signal Processing
comments: 20 pages, 14 figures, submitted to IEEE Signal Processing
abstract: Wavelet scattering networks, which are convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with fixed filters and weights, are promising tools for image analysis. Imposing symmetry on image statistics can improve human interpretability, aid in generalization, and provide dimension reduction. In this work, we introduce a fast-to-compute, translationally invariant and rotationally equivariant wavelet scattering network (EqWS) and filter bank of wavelets (triglets). We demonstrate the interpretability and quantify the invariance/equivariance of the coefficients, briefly commenting on difficulties with implementing scale equivariance. On MNIST, we show that training on a rotationally invariant reduction of the coefficients maintains rotational invariance when generalized to test data and visualize residual symmetry breaking terms. Rotation equivariance is leveraged to estimate the rotation angle of digits and reconstruct the full rotation dependence of each coefficient from a single angle. We benchmark EqWS with linear classifiers on EMNIST and CIFAR-10/100, introducing a new second-order, cross-color channel coupling for the color images. We conclude by comparing the performance of an isotropic reduction of the scattering coefficients and RWST, a previous coefficient reduction, on an isotropic classification of magnetohydrodynamic simulations with astrophysical relevance.

Imposing symmetry on image statistics can improve human interpretability, aid in generalization, and provide dimension reduction. In this work, we introduce a fast-to-compute, translationally invariant and rotationally equivariant wavelet scattering network (EqWS) and filter bank of wavelets (triglets). We demonstrate the interpretability and quantify the invariance/equivariance of the coefficients, briefly commenting on difficulties with implementing scale equivariance. On MNIST, we show that training on a rotationally invariant reduction of the coefficients maintains rotational invariance when generalized to test data and visualize residual symmetry breaking terms. Rotation equivariance is leveraged to estimate the rotation angle of digits and reconstruct the full rotation dependence of each coefficient from a single angle. We benchmark EqWS with linear classifiers on EMNIST and CIFAR-10/100, introducing a new second-order, cross-color channel coupling for the color images. We conclude by comparing the performance of an isotropic reduction of the scattering coefficients and RWST, a previous coefficient reduction, on an isotropic classification of magnetohydrodynamic simulations with astrophysical relevance.
abstract_zh: 小波散射网络是具有固定滤波器和权重的卷积神经网络 (CNN)，是很有前途的图像分析工具。对图像统计施加对称性可以提高人类的可解释性，有助于泛化，并提供降维功能。在这项工作中，我们引入了一种快速计算、平移不变且旋转等变的小波散射网络（EqWS）和小波滤波器组（triglet）。我们证明了可解释性并量化了系数的不变性/等方差，简要评论了实现尺度等方差的困难。在 MNIST 上，我们表明，当推广到测试数据和可视化残差对称性破缺项时，对系数的旋转不变约简进行训练可以保持旋转不变性。利用旋转等方差来估计数字的旋转角度并从单个角度重建每个系数的完整旋转依赖性。我们在 EMNIST 和 CIFAR-10/100 上使用线性分类器对 EqWS 进行基准测试，为彩色图像引入新的二阶跨颜色通道耦合。我们通过比较散射系数的各向同性降低和 RWST（先前的系数降低）对具有天体物理相关性的磁流体动力学模拟的各向同性分类的性能得出结论。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种快速计算的旋转和平移不变波let散射网络(EqWS)，并引入三角小波滤波器，用于图像分析中的对称性建模。
AI.motivation: 通过对称性约束提升卷积神经网络的可解释性、泛化能力和维度缩减效果，解决传统方法在尺度对称性实现中的困难。
AI.method: 设计旋转等变且平移不变的EqWS网络与三角小波滤波器，量化系数的不变性/等变性，利用旋转等变性估计数字旋转角度，并引入跨颜色通道耦合处理彩色图像。
AI.result: 在MNIST上验证旋转不变性保持性，可视化残余对称性破坏项；在EMNIST和CIFAR-10/100数据集上通过线性分类器验证性能；在磁流体动力学模拟中比较各系数约简方法效果。
AI.conclusion: EqWS在保持对称性的同时提升分类性能，证明了旋转等变性在图像分析中的有效性，且各向同性系数约简方法在特定任务中优于传统方法。

authors_text: Tekin Evrim Ozmermer,Viktors Roze,Stanislavs Hilcuks,Alina Nescerecka
title: VeriMedi: Pill Identification using Proxy-based Deep Metric Learning and Exact Solution
title_zh: VeriMedi：使用基于代理的深度度量学习和精确解决方案进行药丸识别
abstract: We present the system that we have developed for the identification and verification of pills using images that are taken by the VeriMedi device. The VeriMedi device is an Internet of Things device that takes pictures of a filled pill vial from the bottom of the vial and uses the solution that is presented in this research to identify the pills in the vials. The solution has two serially connected deep learning solutions which do segmentation and identification. The segmentation solution creates the masks for each pill in the vial image by using the Mask R-CNN model, then segments and crops the pills and blurs the background. After that, the segmented pill images are sent to the identification solution where a Deep Metric Learning model that is trained with Proxy Anchor Loss (PAL) function generates embedding vectors for each pill image. The generated embedding vectors are fed into a one-layer fully connected network that is trained with the exact solution to predict each single pill image. Then, the aggregation/verification function aggregates the multiple predictions coming from multiple single pill images and verifies the correctness of the final prediction with respect to predefined rules. Besides, we enhanced the PAL with a better proxy initialization that increased the performance of the models and let the model learn the new classes of images continually without retraining the model with the whole dataset. When the model that is trained with initial classes is retrained only with new classes, the accuracy of the model increases for both old and new classes. The identification solution that we have presented in this research can also be reused for other problem domains which require continual learning and/or Fine-Grained Visual Categorization.
summary: We present the system that we have developed for the identification and verification of pills using images that are taken by the VeriMedi device. The VeriMedi device is an Internet of Things device that takes pictures of a filled pill vial from the bottom of the vial and uses the solution that is presented in this research to identify the pills in the vials. The solution has two serially connected deep learning solutions which do segmentation and identification. The segmentation solution creates the masks for each pill in the vial image by using the Mask R-CNN model, then segments and crops the pills and blurs the background. After that, the segmented pill images are sent to the identification solution where a Deep Metric Learning model that is trained with Proxy Anchor Loss (PAL) function generates embedding vectors for each pill image. The generated embedding vectors are fed into a one-layer fully connected network that is trained with the exact solution to predict each single pill image. Then, the aggregation/verification function aggregates the multiple predictions coming from multiple single pill images and verifies the correctness of the final prediction with respect to predefined rules. Besides, we enhanced the PAL with a better proxy initialization that increased the performance of the models and let the model learn the new classes of images continually without retraining the model with the whole dataset. When the model that is trained with initial classes is retrained only with new classes, the accuracy of the model increases for both old and new classes. The identification solution that we have presented in this research can also be reused for other problem domains which require continual learning and/or Fine-Grained Visual Categorization.
abstract_zh: 我们展示了我们开发的系统，用于使用 VeriMedi 设备拍摄的图像来识别和验证药丸。 VeriMedi 设备是一种物联网设备，可以从药瓶底部拍摄装满药瓶的照片，并使用本研究中提出的解决方案来识别药瓶中的药丸。该解决方案有两个串联的深度学习解决方案，分别进行分割和识别。分割解决方案使用 Mask R-CNN 模型为小瓶图像中的每个药丸创建掩模，然后分割和裁剪药丸并模糊背景。之后，分割的药丸图像被发送到识别解决方案，其中使用代理锚定损失 (PAL) 函数训练的深度度量学习模型为每个药丸图像生成嵌入向量。生成的嵌入向量被输入一个单层完全连接的网络，该网络使用精确的解决方案进行训练，以预测每个药丸图像。然后，聚合/验证功能聚合来自多个单药丸图像的多个预测，并验证最终预测相对于预定义规则的正确性。此外，我们通过更好的代理初始化增强了 PAL，从而提高了模型的性能，并让模型不断学习新的图像类别，而无需使用整个数据集重新训练模型。当使用初始类训练的模型仅使用新类进行重新训练时，新旧类的模型准确性都会提高。我们在本研究中提出的识别解决方案也可以重复用于需要持续学习和/或细粒度视觉分类的其他问题领域。

AI.tldr: 本文提出VeriMedi系统，通过IoT设备拍摄药瓶图像，结合分割与深度度量学习实现药片识别与验证。
AI.motivation: 为解决药瓶内药片识别的准确性和自动化需求，避免用药错误，同时应对药片重叠、背景复杂等挑战。
AI.method: 采用Mask R-CNN进行药片分割并生成掩膜，利用改进的Proxy Anchor Loss模型生成嵌入向量，通过精确解全连接网络预测药片类别，并通过持续学习机制优化新旧类别识别性能。
AI.result: 模型在旧类和新类上的准确率均提升，且方法可迁移至其他需要细粒度分类和持续学习的领域。
AI.conclusion: 提出的系统有效提升了药片识别的准确性与适应性，改进的代理初始化方法为持续学习提供了新思路。

The identification solution that we have presented in this research can also be reused for other problem domains which require continual learning and/or Fine-Grained Visual Categorization. 我们展示了我们开发的系统，用于使用 VeriMedi 设备拍摄的图像来识别和验证药丸。 VeriMedi 设备是一种物联网设备，可以从药瓶底部拍摄装满药瓶的照片，并使用本研究中提出的解决方案来识别药瓶中的药丸。该解决方案有两个串联的深度学习解决方案，分别进行分割和识别。分割解决方案使用 Mask R-CNN 模型为小瓶图像中的每个药丸创建掩模，然后分割和裁剪药丸并模糊背景。之后，分割的药丸图像被发送到识别解决方案，其中使用代理锚定损失 (PAL) 函数训练的深度度量学习模型为每个药丸图像生成嵌入向量。生成的嵌入向量被输入一个单层完全连接的网络，该网络使用精确的解决方案进行训练，以预测每个药丸图像。然后，聚合/验证功能聚合来自多个单药丸图像的多个预测，并验证最终预测相对于预定义规则的正确性。此外，我们通过更好的代理初始化增强了 PAL，从而提高了模型的性能，并让模型不断学习新的图像类别，而无需使用整个数据集重新训练模型。当使用初始类训练的模型仅使用新类进行重新训练时，新旧类的模型准确性都会提高。我们在本研究中提出的识别解决方案也可以重复用于需要持续学习和/或细粒度视觉分类的其他问题领域。 本文提出VeriMedi系统，通过IoT设备拍摄药瓶图像，结合分割与深度度量学习实现药片识别与验证。 Tekin Evrim Ozmermer,Viktors Roze,Stanislavs Hilcuks,Alina Nescerecka cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.IT,cs.LG

authors_text: Jiajie Wu
title: Literature review on vulnerability detection using NLP technology
title_zh: 利用NLP技术进行漏洞检测的文献综述
abstract: Vulnerability detection has always been the most important task in the field of software security. With the development of technology, in the face of massive source code, automated analysis and detection of vulnerabilities has become a current research hotspot. For special text files such as source code, using some of the hottest NLP technologies to build models and realize the automatic analysis and detection of source code has become one of the most anticipated studies in the field of vulnerability detection. This article does a brief survey of some recent new documents and technologies, such as CodeBERT, and summarizes the previous technologies.
summary: Vulnerability detection has always been the most important task in the field of software security. With the development of technology, in the face of massive source code, automated analysis and detection of vulnerabilities has become a current research hotspot. For special text files such as source code, using some of the hottest NLP technologies to build models and realize the automatic analysis and detection of source code has become one of the most anticipated studies in the field of vulnerability detection. This article does a brief survey of some recent new documents and technologies, such as CodeBERT, and summarizes the previous technologies.
abstract_zh: 漏洞检测一直是软件安全领域最重要的任务。随着技术的发展，面对海量的源代码，漏洞的自动化分析和检测已成为当前的研究热点。针对源代码等特殊文本文件，利用一些最热门的NLP技术构建模型，实现对源代码的自动分析检测已经成为漏洞检测领域最值得期待的研究之一。本文对最近的一些新文献和技术（例如CodeBERT）做了一个简单的回顾，并对之前的技术进行了总结。

AI.tldr: 综述利用NLP技术进行漏洞检测的最新方法与技术
AI.motivation: 随着代码量增加，传统方法效率不足，需探索NLP技术提升自动化检测能力
AI.method: 通过文献综述分析近期研究，总结NLP模型如CodeBERT在漏洞检测中的应用
AI.result: 总结了NLP在漏洞检测中的应用现状，指出现有技术的局限性与未来方向
AI.conclusion: 需优化模型性能并提升跨编程语言的适应性以推动该领域发展

authors_text: Can Wang,Menglei Chai,Mingming He,Dongdong Chen,Jing Liao
title: Cross-Domain and Disentangled Face Manipulation with 3D Guidance
title_zh: 利用 3D 引导进行跨域和解开的面部操作
abstract: Face image manipulation via three-dimensional guidance has been widely applied in various interactive scenarios due to its semantically-meaningful understanding and user-friendly controllability. However, existing 3D-morphable-model-based manipulation methods are not directly applicable to out-of-domain faces, such as non-photorealistic paintings, cartoon portraits, or even animals, mainly due to the formidable difficulties in building the model for each specific face domain. To overcome this challenge, we propose, as far as we know, the first method to manipulate faces in arbitrary domains using human 3DMM. This is achieved through two major steps: 1) disentangled mapping from 3DMM parameters to the latent space embedding of a pre-trained StyleGAN2 that guarantees disentangled and precise controls for each semantic attribute; and 2) cross-domain adaptation that bridges domain discrepancies and makes human 3DMM applicable to out-of-domain faces by enforcing a consistent latent space embedding. Experiments and comparisons demonstrate the superiority of our high-quality semantic manipulation method on a variety of face domains with all major 3D facial attributes controllable-pose, expression, shape, albedo, and illumination. Moreover, we develop an intuitive editing interface to support user-friendly control and instant feedback. Our project page is https://cassiepython.github.io/cddfm3d/index.html
summary: Face image manipulation via three-dimensional guidance has been widely applied in various interactive scenarios due to its semantically-meaningful understanding and user-friendly controllability. However, existing 3D-morphable-model-based manipulation methods are not directly applicable to out-of-domain faces, such as non-photorealistic paintings, cartoon portraits, or even animals, mainly due to the formidable difficulties in building the model for each specific face domain. To overcome this challenge, we propose, as far as we know, the first method to manipulate faces in arbitrary domains using human 3DMM. This is achieved through two major steps: 1) disentangled mapping from 3DMM parameters to the latent space embedding of a pre-trained StyleGAN2 that guarantees disentangled and precise controls for each semantic attribute; and 2) cross-domain adaptation that bridges domain discrepancies and makes human 3DMM applicable to out-of-domain faces by enforcing a consistent latent space embedding. Experiments and comparisons demonstrate the superiority of our high-quality semantic manipulation method on a variety of face domains with all major 3D facial attributes controllable-pose, expression, shape, albedo, and illumination. Moreover, we develop an intuitive editing interface to support user-friendly control and instant feedback. Our project page is https://cassiepython.github.io/cddfm3d/index.html
abstract_zh: 基于三维引导的人脸图像处理因其语义意义的理解和用户友好的可控性而被广泛应用于各种交互场景。然而，现有的基于 3D 变形模型的操纵方法并不直接适用于域外人脸，例如非真实感绘画、卡通肖像甚至动物，这主要是因为为每个特定人脸域构建模型存在巨大困难。为了克服这一挑战，据我们所知，我们提出了第一种使用人类 3DMM 在任意域中操纵人脸的方法。这是通过两个主要步骤实现的：1）从 3DMM 参数到预训练 StyleGAN2 的潜在空间嵌入的解缠映射，保证对每个语义属性的解缠和精确控制； 2) 跨域适应，通过强制执行一致的潜在空间嵌入，弥合域差异并使人类 3DMM 适用于域外人脸。实验和比较证明了我们的高质量语义操作方法在各种面部领域的优越性，所有主要 3D 面部属性均可控制——姿势、表情、形状、反照率和照明。此外，我们开发了直观的编辑界面来支持用户友好的控制和即时反馈。我们的项目页面是 https://cassiepython.github.io/cddfm3d/index.html

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于3D引导的跨领域和解耦人脸编辑方法，能够操控非真实感绘画、卡通等非人脸领域的面部属性。
AI.motivation: 现有基于3D形态模型的人脸编辑方法无法直接处理非人脸领域（如卡通、绘画），因领域差异导致模型构建困难。
AI.method: 1) 构建3DMM参数到StyleGAN2潜在空间的解耦映射，实现语义属性独立控制；2) 通过跨领域适配消除领域差异，使3DMM适用于非人脸领域。
AI.result: 在多种人脸领域（含非真实感图像）实现高质量语义编辑，支持姿态、表情等五大属性控制，并提供直观交互界面。
AI.conclusion: 该方法首次实现跨领域人脸编辑，通过解耦映射与领域适配技术，有效解决了非人脸领域的编辑难题。

To overcome this challenge, we propose, as far as we know, the first method to manipulate faces in arbitrary domains using human 3DMM. authors_text: Haoqi Fan,Bo Xiong,Karttikeya Mangalam,Yanghao Li,Zhicheng Yan,Jitendra Malik,Christoph Feichtenhofer
title: Multiscale Vision Transformers
title_zh: 多尺度视觉变压器
abstract: We present Multiscale Vision Transformers (MViT) for video and image recognition, by connecting the seminal idea of multiscale feature hierarchies with transformer models. Multiscale Transformers have several channel-resolution scale stages. Starting from the input resolution and a small channel dimension, the stages hierarchically expand the channel capacity while reducing the spatial resolution. This creates a multiscale pyramid of features with early layers operating at high spatial resolution to model simple low-level visual information, and deeper layers at spatially coarse, but complex, high-dimensional features. We evaluate this fundamental architectural prior for modeling the dense nature of visual signals for a variety of video recognition tasks where it outperforms concurrent vision transformers that rely on large scale external pre-training and are 5-10x more costly in computation and parameters. We further remove the temporal dimension and apply our model for image classification where it outperforms prior work on vision transformers. Code is available at: https://github.com/facebookresearch/SlowFast
summary: We present Multiscale Vision Transformers (MViT) for video and image recognition, by connecting the seminal idea of multiscale feature hierarchies with transformer models. Multiscale Transformers have several channel-resolution scale stages. Starting from the input resolution and a small channel dimension, the stages hierarchically expand the channel capacity while reducing the spatial resolution. This creates a multiscale pyramid of features with early layers operating at high spatial resolution to model simple low-level visual information, and deeper layers at spatially coarse, but complex, high-dimensional features. We evaluate this fundamental architectural prior for modeling the dense nature of visual signals for a variety of video recognition tasks where it outperforms concurrent vision transformers that rely on large scale external pre-training and are 5-10x more costly in computation and parameters. We further remove the temporal dimension and apply our model for image classification where it outperforms prior work on vision transformers. Code is available at: https://github.com/facebookresearch/SlowFast
abstract_zh: 我们通过将多尺度特征层次结构的开创性思想与变压器模型联系起来，提出了用于视频和图像识别的多尺度视觉变压器（MViT）。多尺度 Transformer 有多个通道分辨率尺度阶段。从输入分辨率和小通道尺寸开始，各阶段分层扩展通道容量，同时降低空间分辨率。这创建了一个多尺度的特征金字塔，早期层以高空间分辨率运行，以模拟简单的低级视觉信息，而更深层则以空间粗糙但复杂的高维特征运行。我们先评估这种基本架构，以对各种视频识别任务的视觉信号的密集性质进行建模，其中它的性能优于依赖大规模外部预训练的并发视觉变换器，并且计算和参数成本高出 5-10 倍。我们进一步消除了时间维度，并将我们的模型应用于图像分类，它的性能优于视觉转换器的先前工作。代码位于：https://github.com/facebookresearch/SlowFast

AI.tldr: 本文提出多尺度视觉变换器（MViT），通过结合多尺度特征层次结构与变换器模型，用于视频和图像识别任务。
AI.motivation: 现有视觉变换器依赖大规模外部预训练且计算成本高，本文旨在通过多尺度特征层次结构设计更高效的模型，以处理视觉信号的密集性。
AI.method: MViT采用多阶段通道-分辨率缩放结构，从低通道高分辨率开始，逐层扩展通道并降低空间分辨率，形成多尺度特征金字塔，同时移除时间维度用于图像分类。
AI.result: 在视频识别任务中，MViT优于依赖大规模预训练的视觉变换器，计算量和参数减少5-10倍；在图像分类任务中也超越了先前方法。
AI.conclusion: 多尺度特征层次结构是建模视觉信号的有效架构先验，MViT在效率和性能上均优于现有方法。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出多尺度视觉变换器（MViT），通过结合多尺度特征层次结构与变换器模型，用于视频和图像识别任务。
ai_content.motivation: 现有视觉变换器依赖大规模外部预训练且计算成本高，本文旨在通过多尺度特征层次结构设计更高效的模型，以处理视觉信号的密集性。
ai_content.method: MViT采用多阶段通道-分辨率缩放结构，从低通道高分辨率开始，逐层扩展通道并降低空间分辨率，形成多尺度特征金字塔，同时移除时间维度用于图像分类。
ai_content.result: 在视频识别任务中，MViT优于依赖大规模预训练的视觉变换器，计算量和参数减少5-10倍；在图像分类任务中也超越了先前方法。
ai_content.conclusion: 多尺度特征层次结构是建模视觉信号的有效架构先验，MViT在效率和性能上均优于现有方法。

search_text: Multiscale Vision Transformers 多尺度视觉变压器 We present Multiscale Vision Transformers (MViT) for video and image recognition, by connecting the seminal idea of multiscale feature hierarchies with transformer models. Multiscale Transformers have several channel-resolution scale stages. Starting from the input resolution and a small channel dimension, the stages hierarchically expand the channel capacity while reducing the spatial resolution. This creates a multiscale pyramid of features with early layers operating at high spatial resolution to model simple low-level visual information, and deeper layers at spatially coarse, but complex, high-dimensional features. We evaluate this fundamental architectural prior for modeling the dense nature of visual signals for a variety of video recognition tasks where it outperforms concurrent vision transformers that rely on large scale external pre-training and are 5-10x more costly in computation and parameters. We further remove the temporal dimension and apply our model for image classification where it outperforms prior work on vision transformers. Code is available at: https://github.com/facebookresearch/SlowFast 我们通过将多尺度特征层次结构的开创性思想与变压器模型联系起来，提出了用于视频和图像识别的多尺度视觉变压器（MViT）。多尺度 Transformer 有多个通道分辨率尺度阶段。从输入分辨率和小通道尺寸开始，各阶段分层扩展通道容量，同时降低空间分辨率。这创建了一个多尺度的特征金字塔，早期层以高空间分辨率运行，以模拟简单的低级视觉信息，而更深层则以空间粗糙但复杂的高维特征运行。我们先评估这种基本架构，以对各种视频识别任务的视觉信号的密集性质进行建模，其中它的性能优于依赖大规模外部预训练的并发视觉变换器，并且计算和参数成本高出 5-10 倍。我们进一步消除了时间维度，并将我们的模型应用于图像分类，它的性能优于视觉转换器的先前工作。代码位于：https://github.com/facebookresearch/SlowFast 本文提出多尺度视觉变换器（MViT），通过结合多尺度特征层次结构与变换器模型，用于视频和图像识别任务。 Haoqi Fan,Bo Xiong,Karttikeya Mangalam,Yanghao Li,Zhicheng Yan,Jitendra Malik,Christoph Feichtenhofer cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Ji Hou,Saining Xie,Benjamin Graham,Angela Dai,Matthias Nießner
title: Pri3D: Can 3D Priors Help 2D Representation Learning?
title_zh: Pri3D：3D 先验可以帮助 2D 表示学习吗？
abstract: Recent advances in 3D perception have shown impressive progress in understanding geometric structures of 3Dshapes and even scenes. Inspired by these advances in geometric understanding, we aim to imbue image-based perception with representations learned under geometric constraints. We introduce an approach to learn view-invariant,geometry-aware representations for network pre-training, based on multi-view RGB-D data, that can then be effectively transferred to downstream 2D tasks. We propose to employ contrastive learning under both multi-view im-age constraints and image-geometry constraints to encode3D priors into learned 2D representations. This results not only in improvement over 2D-only representation learning on the image-based tasks of semantic segmentation, instance segmentation, and object detection on real-world in-door datasets, but moreover, provides significant improvement in the low data regime. We show a significant improvement of 6.0% on semantic segmentation on full data as well as 11.9% on 20% data against baselines on ScanNet.
summary: Recent advances in 3D perception have shown impressive progress in understanding geometric structures of 3Dshapes and even scenes. Inspired by these advances in geometric understanding, we aim to imbue image-based perception with representations learned under geometric constraints. We introduce an approach to learn view-invariant,geometry-aware representations for network pre-training, based on multi-view RGB-D data, that can then be effectively transferred to downstream 2D tasks. We propose to employ contrastive learning under both multi-view im-age constraints and image-geometry constraints to encode3D priors into learned 2D representations. This results not only in improvement over 2D-only representation learning on the image-based tasks of semantic segmentation, instance segmentation, and object detection on real-world in-door datasets, but moreover, provides significant improvement in the low data regime. We show a significant improvement of 6.0% on semantic segmentation on full data as well as 11.9% on 20% data against baselines on ScanNet.
abstract_zh: 3D 感知的最新进展在理解 3D 形状甚至场景的几何结构方面取得了令人瞩目的进展。受到几何理解方面这些进步的启发，我们的目标是将在几何约束下学习的表示注入基于图像的感知。我们引入了一种基于多视图 RGB-D 数据来学习网络预训练的视图不变、几何感知表示的方法，然后可以将其有效地转移到下游 2D 任务。我们建议在多视图图像约束和图像几何约束下采用对比学习，将 3D 先验编码为学习的 2D 表示。这不仅在基于图像的语义分割、实例分割和真实室内数据集的对象检测任务上比纯二维表示学习得到了改进，而且在低数据状态下提供了显着的改进。我们发现，与 ScanNet 的基线相比，完整数据的语义分割显着提高了 6.0%，20% 数据的语义分割显着提高了 11.9%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Pri3D方法，通过引入3D先验知识提升2D表示学习效果，利用多视角RGB-D数据和对比学习构建几何感知的图像表示。
AI.motivation: 传统2D表示学习缺乏对几何结构的建模能力，在数据稀缺场景下表现受限，而3D感知技术的进步为融合几何先验提供了新思路。
AI.method: 基于多视角RGB-D数据，通过同时施加图像约束和几何约束的对比学习，将3D先验编码到2D表示中，实现跨视角不变的几何感知表征学习。
AI.result: 在ScanNet数据集上，语义分割任务全数据下提升6.0%，20%数据下提升11.9%，显著优于基线方法，且在实例分割和目标检测任务中均有提升。
AI.conclusion: 融合3D先验的表示学习能有效增强2D视觉任务性能，尤其在低数据场景下优势显著，为跨模态表征学习提供新方向。

authors_text: Tomas Jakab,Richard Tucker,Ameesh Makadia,Jiajun Wu,Noah Snavely,Angjoo Kanazawa
title: KeypointDeformer: Unsupervised 3D Keypoint Discovery for Shape Control
title_zh: KeypointDeformer：用于形状控制的无监督 3D 关键点发现
abstract: We introduce KeypointDeformer, a novel unsupervised method for shape control through automatically discovered 3D keypoints. We cast this as the problem of aligning a source 3D object to a target 3D object from the same object category. Our method analyzes the difference between the shapes of the two objects by comparing their latent representations. This latent representation is in the form of 3D keypoints that are learned in an unsupervised way. The difference between the 3D keypoints of the source and the target objects then informs the shape deformation algorithm that deforms the source object into the target object. The whole model is learned end-to-end and simultaneously discovers 3D keypoints while learning to use them for deforming object shapes. Our approach produces intuitive and semantically consistent control of shape deformations. Moreover, our discovered 3D keypoints are consistent across object category instances despite large shape variations. As our method is unsupervised, it can be readily deployed to new object categories without requiring annotations for 3D keypoints and deformations.
summary: We introduce KeypointDeformer, a novel unsupervised method for shape control through automatically discovered 3D keypoints. We cast this as the problem of aligning a source 3D object to a target 3D object from the same object category. Our method analyzes the difference between the shapes of the two objects by comparing their latent representations. This latent representation is in the form of 3D keypoints that are learned in an unsupervised way. The difference between the 3D keypoints of the source and the target objects then informs the shape deformation algorithm that deforms the source object into the target object. The whole model is learned end-to-end and simultaneously discovers 3D keypoints while learning to use them for deforming object shapes. Our approach produces intuitive and semantically consistent control of shape deformations. Moreover, our discovered 3D keypoints are consistent across object category instances despite large shape variations. As our method is unsupervised, it can be readily deployed to new object categories without requiring annotations for 3D keypoints and deformations.
abstract_zh: 我们引入了 KeypointDeformer，这是一种通过自动发现的 3D 关键点进行形状控制的新型无监督方法。我们将此视为将源 3D 对象与同一对象类别中的目标 3D 对象对齐的问题。我们的方法通过比较两个对象的潜在表示来分析它们的形状之间的差异。这种潜在表示采用 3D 关键点的形式，以无监督的方式学习。然后，源对象和目标对象的 3D 关键点之间的差异会通知形状变形算法，将源对象变形为目标对象。整个模型是端到端学习的，同时发现 3D 关键点，同时学习使用它们来使对象形状变形。我们的方法对形状变形产生直观且语义一致的控制。此外，我们发现的 3D 关键点在对象类别实例之间是一致的，尽管形状变化很大。由于我们的方法是无监督的，因此它可以轻松部署到新的对象类别，而不需要 3D 关键点和变形的注释。

AI.tldr: 本文提出KeypointDeformer，一种通过自动发现的3D关键点实现形状控制的无监督方法。
AI.motivation: 现有3D形状控制方法依赖人工标注的关键点和变形信息，而本文旨在无需标注即可自动发现关键点并实现跨实例的形状变形。
AI.method: 通过比较源对象与目标对象的潜在表示（3D关键点）差异，驱动形状变形算法，整个模型端到端学习关键点发现与变形控制。
AI.result: 生成直观且语义一致的形状变形，发现的关键点在同类物体不同实例间保持一致，且无需标注即可适配新类别。
AI.conclusion: 该方法突破了传统标注依赖，为3D形状控制提供了通用解决方案，具有广泛的应用前景。

Our approach produces intuitive and semantically consistent control of shape deformations. Moreover, our discovered 3D keypoints are consistent across object category instances despite large shape variations. As our method is unsupervised, it can be readily deployed to new object categories without requiring annotations for 3D keypoints and deformations. 我们引入了 KeypointDeformer，这是一种通过自动发现的 3D 关键点进行形状控制的新型无监督方法。我们将此视为将源 3D 对象与同一对象类别中的目标 3D 对象对齐的问题。我们的方法通过比较两个对象的潜在表示来分析它们的形状之间的差异。这种潜在表示采用 3D 关键点的形式，以无监督的方式学习。然后，源对象和目标对象的 3D 关键点之间的差异会通知形状变形算法，将源对象变形为目标对象。整个模型是端到端学习的，同时发现 3D 关键点，同时学习使用它们来使对象形状变形。我们的方法对形状变形产生直观且语义一致的控制。此外，我们发现的 3D 关键点在对象类别实例之间是一致的，尽管形状变化很大。由于我们的方法是无监督的，因此它可以轻松部署到新的对象类别，而不需要 3D 关键点和变形的注释。 本文提出KeypointDeformer，一种通过自动发现的3D关键点实现形状控制的无监督方法。 Tomas Jakab,Richard Tucker,Ameesh Makadia,Jiajun Wu,Noah Snavely,Angjoo Kanazawa cs.CV,cs.GR

authors_text: Gaurav Parmar,Richard Zhang,Jun-Yan Zhu
title: On Aliased Resizing and Surprising Subtleties in GAN Evaluation
title_zh: 关于 GAN 评估中的别名调整和令人惊讶的微妙之处
abstract: Metrics for evaluating generative models aim to measure the discrepancy between real and generated images. The often-used Frechet Inception Distance (FID) metric, for example, extracts "high-level" features using a deep network from the two sets. However, we find that the differences in "low-level" preprocessing, specifically image resizing and compression, can induce large variations and have unforeseen consequences. For instance, when resizing an image, e.g., with a bilinear or bicubic kernel, signal processing principles mandate adjusting prefilter width depending on the downsampling factor, to antialias to the appropriate bandwidth. However, commonly-used implementations use a fixed-width prefilter, resulting in aliasing artifacts. Such aliasing leads to corruptions in the feature extraction downstream. Next, lossy compression, such as JPEG, is commonly used to reduce the file size of an image. Although designed to minimally degrade the perceptual quality of an image, the operation also produces variations downstream. Furthermore, we show that if compression is used on real training images, FID can actually improve if the generated images are also subsequently compressed. This paper shows that choices in low-level image processing have been an underappreciated aspect of generative modeling. We identify and characterize variations in generative modeling development pipelines, provide recommendations based on signal processing principles, and release a reference implementation to facilitate future comparisons.

The often-used Frechet Inception Distance (FID) metric, for example, extracts "high-level" features using a deep network from the two sets. However, we find that the differences in "low-level" preprocessing, specifically image resizing and compression, can induce large variations and have unforeseen consequences. However, commonly-used implementations use a fixed-width prefilter, resulting in aliasing artifacts. Such aliasing leads to corruptions in the feature extraction downstream. Next, lossy compression, such as JPEG, is commonly used to reduce the file size of an image. Although designed to minimally degrade the perceptual quality of an image, the operation also produces variations downstream. Furthermore, we show that if compression is used on real training images, FID can actually improve if the generated images are also subsequently compressed. This paper shows that choices in low-level image processing have been an underappreciated aspect of generative modeling. We identify and characterize variations in generative modeling development pipelines, provide recommendations based on signal processing principles, and release a reference implementation to facilitate future comparisons.
abstract_zh: 评估生成模型的指标旨在测量真实图像和生成图像之间的差异。例如，常用的弗雷切起始距离 (FID) 度量使用深度网络从两个集合中提取“高级”特征。然而，我们发现“低级”预处理的差异，特别是图像大小调整和压缩，可能会引起很大的变化并产生不可预见的后果。例如，当调整图像大小时，例如使用双线性或双三次内核，信号处理原理要求根据下采样因子调整预滤波器宽度，以抗锯齿到适当的带宽。然而，常用的实现使用固定宽度的预过滤器，从而导致混叠伪影。这种混叠会导致下游特征提取的损坏。其次，有损压缩（例如 JPEG）通常用于减小图像的文件大小。尽管旨在最大限度地降低图像的感知质量，但该操作也会在下游产生变化。此外，我们还表明，如果在真实训练图像上使用压缩，并且生成的图像随后也被压缩，那么 FID 实际上可以得到改进。本文表明，低级图像处理的选择一直是生成建模中未被充分认识的一个方面。我们识别并描述生成建模开发流程中的变化，根据信号处理原理提供建议，并发布参考实现以方便将来进行比较。

AI.tldr: 本文研究生成模型评估中低级图像预处理（如调整大小和压缩）对常用指标（如FID）的影响，并提出改进方法。
AI.motivation: 现有生成模型评估指标（如FID）未充分考虑低级预处理步骤（如调整大小和压缩）带来的偏差，可能导致评估结果不可靠。
AI.method: 通过分析调整大小时预滤波器宽度固定导致的混叠效应，以及JPEG压缩对特征提取的影响，结合实验验证不同预处理方式对FID的影响，并提出符合信号处理原理的改进方案。
AI.result: 发现固定预滤波器宽度会导致混叠伪影，压缩真实图像可能使生成图像的FID分数虚假提升，验证了低级处理步骤对评估结果的显著影响。
AI.conclusion: 低级图像处理步骤是生成模型评估中的关键因素，需遵循信号处理原理标准化预处理流程，并提供参考实现以促进公平比较。

authors_text: Yang Liu,Idil Esen Zulfikar,Jonathon Luiten,Achal Dave,Deva Ramanan,Bastian Leibe,Aljoša Ošep,Laura Leal-Taixé
title: Opening up Open-World Tracking
title_zh: 开放开放世界追踪
abstract: Tracking and detecting any object, including ones never-seen-before during model training, is a crucial but elusive capability of autonomous systems. An autonomous agent that is blind to never-seen-before objects poses a safety hazard when operating in the real world - and yet this is how almost all current systems work. One of the main obstacles towards advancing tracking any object is that this task is notoriously difficult to evaluate. A benchmark that would allow us to perform an apples-to-apples comparison of existing efforts is a crucial first step towards advancing this important research field. This paper addresses this evaluation deficit and lays out the landscape and evaluation methodology for detecting and tracking both known and unknown objects in the open-world setting. We propose a new benchmark, TAO-OW: Tracking Any Object in an Open World, analyze existing efforts in multi-object tracking, and construct a baseline for this task while highlighting future challenges. We hope to open a new front in multi-object tracking research that will hopefully bring us a step closer to intelligent systems that can operate safely in the real world. https://openworldtracking.github.io/
summary: Tracking and detecting any object, including ones never-seen-before during model training, is a crucial but elusive capability of autonomous systems. An autonomous agent that is blind to never-seen-before objects poses a safety hazard when operating in the real world - and yet this is how almost all current systems work. One of the main obstacles towards advancing tracking any object is that this task is notoriously difficult to evaluate. A benchmark that would allow us to perform an apples-to-apples comparison of existing efforts is a crucial first step towards advancing this important research field. This paper addresses this evaluation deficit and lays out the landscape and evaluation methodology for detecting and tracking both known and unknown objects in the open-world setting. We propose a new benchmark, TAO-OW: Tracking Any Object in an Open World, analyze existing efforts in multi-object tracking, and construct a baseline for this task while highlighting future challenges. We hope to open a new front in multi-object tracking research that will hopefully bring us a step closer to intelligent systems that can operate safely in the real world. https://openworldtracking.github.io/
abstract_zh: 跟踪和检测任何物体，包括模型训练期间从未见过的物体，是自主系统的一项至关重要但难以捉摸的能力。对从未见过的物体视而不见的自主代理在现实世界中运行时会带来安全隐患，但这就是几乎所有当前系统的工作方式。推进跟踪任何物体的主要障碍之一是这项任务很难评估。一个使我们能够对现有工作进行同类比较的基准是推进这一重要研究领域的关键的第一步。本文解决了这一评估缺陷，并阐述了在开放世界环境中检测和跟踪已知和未知物体的景观和评估方法。我们提出了一个新的基准，TAO-OW：跟踪开放世界中的任何对象，分析多对象跟踪方面的现有工作，并为此任务构建基线，同时强调未来的挑战。我们希望开辟多目标跟踪研究的新前沿，这有望使我们离能够在现实世界中安全运行的智能系统更近一步。 https://openworldtracking.github.io/

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个名为TAO-OW的新基准，用于评估开放世界中已知和未知物体的检测与跟踪能力。
AI.motivation: 当前自主系统无法识别训练时未见过的物体，这在真实世界中存在安全隐患，而现有跟踪任务缺乏有效评估方法。
AI.method: 构建开放世界跟踪基准TAO-OW，分析现有多种目标跟踪方法，并建立该任务的基线模型。
AI.result: 提出了开放世界跟踪评估框架，分析了现有方法的局限性，并指出了未来研究的关键挑战。
AI.conclusion: 该工作为多目标跟踪研究开辟了新方向，推动智能系统在真实世界中更安全地运行。

authors_text: Sumith Kulal,Jiayuan Mao,Alex Aiken,Jiajun Wu
title: Hierarchical Motion Understanding via Motion Programs
title_zh: 通过运动程序进行分层运动理解
comment: CVPR 2021. First two authors contributed equally. Project page: https://sumith1896.github.io/motion2prog/
comments: CVPR 2021. First two authors contributed equally. Project page: https://sumith1896.github.io/motion2prog/
abstract: Current approaches to video analysis of human motion focus on raw pixels or keypoints as the basic units of reasoning. We posit that adding higher-level motion primitives, which can capture natural coarser units of motion such as backswing or follow-through, can be used to improve downstream analysis tasks. This higher level of abstraction can also capture key features, such as loops of repeated primitives, that are currently inaccessible at lower levels of representation. We therefore introduce Motion Programs, a neuro-symbolic, program-like representation that expresses motions as a composition of high-level primitives. We also present a system for automatically inducing motion programs from videos of human motion and for leveraging motion programs in video synthesis. Experiments show that motion programs can accurately describe a diverse set of human motions and the inferred programs contain semantically meaningful motion primitives, such as arm swings and jumping jacks. Our representation also benefits downstream tasks such as video interpolation and video prediction and outperforms off-the-shelf models. We further demonstrate how these programs can detect diverse kinds of repetitive motion and facilitate interactive video editing.
summary: Current approaches to video analysis of human motion focus on raw pixels or keypoints as the basic units of reasoning. We posit that adding higher-level motion primitives, which can capture natural coarser units of motion such as backswing or follow-through, can be used to improve downstream analysis tasks. This higher level of abstraction can also capture key features, such as loops of repeated primitives, that are currently inaccessible at lower levels of representation. We therefore introduce Motion Programs, a neuro-symbolic, program-like representation that expresses motions as a composition of high-level primitives. We also present a system for automatically inducing motion programs from videos of human motion and for leveraging motion programs in video synthesis. Experiments show that motion programs can accurately describe a diverse set of human motions and the inferred programs contain semantically meaningful motion primitives, such as arm swings and jumping jacks. Our representation also benefits downstream tasks such as video interpolation and video prediction and outperforms off-the-shelf models. We further demonstrate how these programs can detect diverse kinds of repetitive motion and facilitate interactive video editing.
abstract_zh: 当前的人体运动视频分析方法侧重于将原始像素或关键点作为推理的基本单位。我们假设添加更高级别的运动基元，可以捕获自然的更粗糙的运动单位，例如后摆或后续动作，可用于改进下游分析任务。这种更高级别的抽象还可以捕获目前在较低表示级别无法访问的关键特征，例如重复基元的循环。因此，我们引入了运动程序，这是一种神经符号、类似程序的表示形式，将运动表示为高级基元的组合。我们还提出了一个系统，用于从人体运动视频中自动诱导运动程序，并在视频合成中利用运动程序。实验表明，运动程序可以准确地描述多种人类运动，并且推断的程序包含语义上有意义的运动原语，例如手臂摆动和开合跳跃。我们的表示也有利于下游任务，例如视频插值和视频预测，并且优于现成的模型。我们进一步演示了这些程序如何检测各种重复运动并促进交互式视频编辑。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Motion Programs的分层运动表示方法，通过引入高层运动原语来提升视频中人体运动的理解与分析。
AI.motivation: 现有视频分析方法依赖像素或关键点等低层表示，难以捕捉如挥臂或跳跃等高层运动模式及重复动作特征。
AI.method: 设计神经符号化的Motion Programs框架，将运动分解为高层原语组合，并构建自动从视频中诱导运动程序的系统，用于视频合成与编辑。
AI.result: 实验表明，Motion Programs能准确描述多样化人体运动，包含语义明确的原语（如跳跃运动），并在视频插值、预测等任务中优于现有模型。
AI.conclusion: Motion Programs通过分层抽象提升了运动理解能力，为视频分析与生成提供了新的有效表示方法。

search_text: Hierarchical Motion Understanding via Motion Programs 通过运动程序进行分层运动理解 Current approaches to video analysis of human motion focus on raw pixels or keypoints as the basic units of reasoning. We posit that adding higher-level motion primitives, which can capture natural coarser units of motion such as backswing or follow-through, can be used to improve downstream analysis tasks. This higher level of abstraction can also capture key features, such as loops of repeated primitives, that are currently inaccessible at lower levels of representation. We therefore introduce Motion Programs, a neuro-symbolic, program-like representation that expresses motions as a composition of high-level primitives. We also present a system for automatically inducing motion programs from videos of human motion and for leveraging motion programs in video synthesis. Experiments show that motion programs can accurately describe a diverse set of human motions and the inferred programs contain semantically meaningful motion primitives, such as arm swings and jumping jacks. Our representation also benefits downstream tasks such as video interpolation and video prediction and outperforms off-the-shelf models. We further demonstrate how these programs can detect diverse kinds of repetitive motion and facilitate interactive video editing. 当前的人体运动视频分析方法侧重于将原始像素或关键点作为推理的基本单位。我们假设添加更高级别的运动基元，可以捕获自然的更粗糙的运动单位，例如后摆或后续动作，可用于改进下游分析任务。这种更高级别的抽象还可以捕获目前在较低表示级别无法访问的关键特征，例如重复基元的循环。因此，我们引入了运动程序，这是一种神经符号、类似程序的表示形式，将运动表示为高级基元的组合。我们还提出了一个系统，用于从人体运动视频中自动诱导运动程序，并在视频合成中利用运动程序。实验表明，运动程序可以准确地描述多种人类运动，并且推断的程序包含语义上有意义的运动原语，例如手臂摆动和开合跳跃。我们的表示也有利于下游任务，例如视频插值和视频预测，并且优于现成的模型。我们进一步演示了这些程序如何检测各种重复运动并促进交互式视频编辑。 本文提出了一种名为Motion Programs的分层运动表示方法，通过引入高层运动原语来提升视频中人体运动的理解与分析。 Sumith Kulal,Jiayuan Mao,Alex Aiken,Jiajun Wu cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG,stat.ML

authors_text: Youjia Zhou,Archit Rathore,Emilie Purvine,Bei Wang
title: Topological Simplifications of Hypergraphs
title_zh: 超图的拓扑简化
abstract: We study hypergraph visualization via its topological simplification. We explore both vertex simplification and hyperedge simplification of hypergraphs using tools from topological data analysis. In particular, we transform a hypergraph to its graph representations known as the line graph and clique expansion. A topological simplification of such a graph representation induces a simplification of the hypergraph. In simplifying a hypergraph, we allow vertices to be combined if they belong to almost the same set of hyperedges, and hyperedges to be merged if they share almost the same set of vertices. Our proposed approaches are general, mathematically justifiable, and they put vertex simplification and hyperedge simplification in a unifying framework.
summary: We study hypergraph visualization via its topological simplification. We explore both vertex simplification and hyperedge simplification of hypergraphs using tools from topological data analysis. In particular, we transform a hypergraph to its graph representations known as the line graph and clique expansion. A topological simplification of such a graph representation induces a simplification of the hypergraph. In simplifying a hypergraph, we allow vertices to be combined if they belong to almost the same set of hyperedges, and hyperedges to be merged if they share almost the same set of vertices. Our proposed approaches are general, mathematically justifiable, and they put vertex simplification and hyperedge simplification in a unifying framework.
abstract_zh: 我们通过拓扑简化来研究超图可视化。我们使用拓扑数据分析工具探索超图的顶点简化和超边简化。特别是，我们将超图转换为其图形表示形式，称为线图和团扩展。这种图表示的拓扑简化导致了超图的简化。在简化超图时，如果顶点属于几乎同一组超边，则我们允许将它们合并；如果它们共享几乎同一组顶点，则允许将超边合并。我们提出的方法是通用的，在数学上是合理的，并且它们将顶点简化和超边简化放在一个统一的框架中。

AI.tldr: 本文研究通过拓扑简化方法对超图结构进行简化，以促进其可视化。
AI.motivation: 传统超图可视化方法复杂且难以处理大规模数据，需更有效的简化方法。
AI.method: 利用拓扑数据分析工具，将超图转化为线图和团扩展图，通过合并相似顶点和超边实现简化。
AI.result: 提出的方法在数学上合理且通用，为顶点和超边简化提供了统一框架。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效简化超图结构，为复杂数据的可视化分析提供了新途径。

authors_text: Kiana Ehsani,Winson Han,Alvaro Herrasti,Eli VanderBilt,Luca Weihs,Eric Kolve,Aniruddha Kembhavi,Roozbeh Mottaghi
title: ManipulaTHOR: A Framework for Visual Object Manipulation
title_zh: ManipulaTHOR：视觉对象操作框架
abstract: The domain of Embodied AI has recently witnessed substantial progress, particularly in navigating agents within their environments. These early successes have laid the building blocks for the community to tackle tasks that require agents to actively interact with objects in their environment. Object manipulation is an established research domain within the robotics community and poses several challenges including manipulator motion, grasping and long-horizon planning, particularly when dealing with oft-overlooked practical setups involving visually rich and complex scenes, manipulation using mobile agents (as opposed to tabletop manipulation), and generalization to unseen environments and objects. We propose a framework for object manipulation built upon the physics-enabled, visually rich AI2-THOR framework and present a new challenge to the Embodied AI community known as ArmPointNav. This task extends the popular point navigation task to object manipulation and offers new challenges including 3D obstacle avoidance, manipulating objects in the presence of occlusion, and multi-object manipulation that necessitates long term planning. Popular learning paradigms that are successful on PointNav challenges show promise, but leave a large room for improvement.
summary: The domain of Embodied AI has recently witnessed substantial progress, particularly in navigating agents within their environments. These early successes have laid the building blocks for the community to tackle tasks that require agents to actively interact with objects in their environment. Object manipulation is an established research domain within the robotics community and poses several challenges including manipulator motion, grasping and long-horizon planning, particularly when dealing with oft-overlooked practical setups involving visually rich and complex scenes, manipulation using mobile agents (as opposed to tabletop manipulation), and generalization to unseen environments and objects. We propose a framework for object manipulation built upon the physics-enabled, visually rich AI2-THOR framework and present a new challenge to the Embodied AI community known as ArmPointNav. This task extends the popular point navigation task to object manipulation and offers new challenges including 3D obstacle avoidance, manipulating objects in the presence of occlusion, and multi-object manipulation that necessitates long term planning. Popular learning paradigms that are successful on PointNav challenges show promise, but leave a large room for improvement.
abstract_zh: 嵌入式人工智能领域最近取得了重大进展，特别是在环境中导航代理方面。这些早期的成功为社区解决需要智能体与环境中的对象主动交互的任务奠定了基础。对象操纵是机器人社区内的一个既定研究领域，并带来了一些挑战，包括操纵器运动、抓取和长期规划，特别是在处理经常被忽视的涉及视觉丰富和复杂场景的实际设置、使用移动代理进行操纵（而不是桌面操纵）以及对不可见的环境和物体进行泛化时。我们提出了一个基于物理支持、视觉效果丰富的 AI2-THOR 框架的对象操作框架，并向名为 ArmPointNav 的 Embodied AI 社区提出了新的挑战。该任务将流行的点导航任务扩展到对象操纵，并提出了新的挑战，包括 3D 避障、在存在遮挡的情况下操纵对象以及需要长期规划的多对象操纵。在 PointNav 挑战中取得成功的流行学习范例显示出了希望，但仍有很大的改进空间。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ManipulaTHOR框架和ArmPointNav任务，旨在解决视觉物体操作中的复杂挑战，如3D避障、遮挡处理及多物体长期规划。
AI.motivation: 现有具身AI在导航任务上取得进展，但物体操作面临移动代理场景下视觉复杂性、遮挡及泛化能力不足等未解决的难题。
AI.method: 基于物理引擎的AI2-THOR框架构建ManipulaTHOR，设计ArmPointNav任务扩展点导航，引入多物体操作与长期规划等新挑战。
AI.result: 现有PointNav方法在新任务中表现有限，验证了ArmPointNav对算法鲁棒性和规划能力的更高要求。
AI.conclusion: ManipulaTHOR为具身AI社区提供了标准化测试平台，推动针对复杂视觉场景下物体操作算法的研究与创新。

We propose a framework for object manipulation built upon the physics-enabled, visually rich AI2-THOR framework and present a new challenge to the Embodied AI community known as ArmPointNav. This task extends the popular point navigation task to object manipulation and offers new challenges including 3D obstacle avoidance, manipulating objects in the presence of occlusion, and multi-object manipulation that necessitates long term planning. Popular learning paradigms that are successful on PointNav challenges show promise, but leave a large room for improvement. 嵌入式人工智能领域最近取得了重大进展，特别是在环境中导航代理方面。这些早期的成功为社区解决需要智能体与环境中的对象主动交互的任务奠定了基础。对象操纵是机器人社区内的一个既定研究领域，并带来了一些挑战，包括操纵器运动、抓取和长期规划，特别是在处理经常被忽视的涉及视觉丰富和复杂场景的实际设置、使用移动代理进行操纵（而不是桌面操纵）以及对不可见的环境和物体进行泛化时。我们提出了一个基于物理支持、视觉效果丰富的 AI2-THOR 框架的对象操作框架，并向名为 ArmPointNav 的 Embodied AI 社区提出了新的挑战。该任务将流行的点导航任务扩展到对象操纵，并提出了新的挑战，包括 3D 避障、在存在遮挡的情况下操纵对象以及需要长期规划的多对象操纵。在 PointNav 挑战中取得成功的流行学习范例显示出了希望，但仍有很大的改进空间。 本文提出ManipulaTHOR框架和ArmPointNav任务，旨在解决视觉物体操作中的复杂挑战，如3D避障、遮挡处理及多物体长期规划。 Kiana Ehsani,Winson Han,Alvaro Herrasti,Eli VanderBilt,Luca Weihs,Eric Kolve,Aniruddha Kembhavi,Roozbeh Mottaghi cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG,cs.RO

authors_text: Yanan Sun,Guanzhi Wang,Qiao Gu,Chi-Keung Tang,Yu-Wing Tai
title: Deep Video Matting via Spatio-Temporal Alignment and Aggregation
title_zh: 通过时空对齐和聚合进行深度视频抠图
abstract: Despite the significant progress made by deep learning in natural image matting, there has been so far no representative work on deep learning for video matting due to the inherent technical challenges in reasoning temporal domain and lack of large-scale video matting datasets. In this paper, we propose a deep learning-based video matting framework which employs a novel and effective spatio-temporal feature aggregation module (ST-FAM). As optical flow estimation can be very unreliable within matting regions, ST-FAM is designed to effectively align and aggregate information across different spatial scales and temporal frames within the network decoder. To eliminate frame-by-frame trimap annotations, a lightweight interactive trimap propagation network is also introduced. The other contribution consists of a large-scale video matting dataset with groundtruth alpha mattes for quantitative evaluation and real-world high-resolution videos with trimaps for qualitative evaluation. Quantitative and qualitative experimental results show that our framework significantly outperforms conventional video matting and deep image matting methods applied to video in presence of multi-frame temporal information.
summary: Despite the significant progress made by deep learning in natural image matting, there has been so far no representative work on deep learning for video matting due to the inherent technical challenges in reasoning temporal domain and lack of large-scale video matting datasets. In this paper, we propose a deep learning-based video matting framework which employs a novel and effective spatio-temporal feature aggregation module (ST-FAM). As optical flow estimation can be very unreliable within matting regions, ST-FAM is designed to effectively align and aggregate information across different spatial scales and temporal frames within the network decoder. To eliminate frame-by-frame trimap annotations, a lightweight interactive trimap propagation network is also introduced. The other contribution consists of a large-scale video matting dataset with groundtruth alpha mattes for quantitative evaluation and real-world high-resolution videos with trimaps for qualitative evaluation. Quantitative and qualitative experimental results show that our framework significantly outperforms conventional video matting and deep image matting methods applied to video in presence of multi-frame temporal information.
abstract_zh: 尽管深度学习在自然图像抠图方面取得了重大进展，但由于时域推理固有的技术挑战以及缺乏大规模视频抠图数据集，迄今为止还没有针对视频抠图的深度学习的代表性工作。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于深度学习的视频抠图框架，该框架采用新颖且有效的时空特征聚合模块（ST-FAM）。由于光流估计在抠图区域内非常不可靠，ST-FAM 旨在有效地对齐和聚合网络解码器内不同空间尺度和时间帧的信息。为了消除逐帧三元图注释，还引入了轻量级交互式三元图传播网络。另一个贡献包括一个大规模视频抠图数据集，其中包含用于定量评估的 groundtruth alpha mattes，以及包含用于定性评估的 trimap 的现实世界高分辨率视频。定量和定性实验结果表明，在存在多帧时间信息的情况下，我们的框架显着优于应用于视频的传统视频抠图和深度图像抠图方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度学习的视频抠图框架，通过时空对齐与聚合模块解决视频抠图中的时序推理难题。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习方法在视频抠图领域缺乏突破，主要受限于时序信息处理难度及高质量视频抠图数据集的缺失。
AI.method: 设计时空特征聚合模块（ST-FAM）实现跨帧信息对齐，引入轻量级三图传播网络减少人工标注依赖，并构建大规模视频抠图数据集。
AI.result: 实验表明该框架在定量指标和视觉效果上均显著优于传统视频抠图方法及图像抠图方法在视频上的应用。
AI.conclusion: 提出的框架有效解决了视频抠图的时序建模难题，为后续研究提供了新思路和基准数据集。

authors_text: Xili Dai,Haigang Gong,Shuai Wu,Xiaojun Yuan,Yi Ma
title: Fully Convolutional Line Parsing
title_zh: 全卷积线解析
abstract: We present a one-stage Fully Convolutional Line Parsing network (F-Clip) that detects line segments from images. The proposed network is very simple and flexible with variations that gracefully trade off between speed and accuracy for different applications. F-Clip detects line segments in an end-to-end fashion by predicting each line's center position, length, and angle. We further customize the design of convolution kernels of our fully convolutional network to effectively exploit the statistical priors of the distribution of line angles in real image datasets. We conduct extensive experiments and show that our method achieves a significantly better trade-off between efficiency and accuracy, resulting in a real-time line detector at up to 73 FPS on a single GPU. Such inference speed makes our method readily applicable to real-time tasks without compromising any accuracy of previous methods. Moreover, when equipped with a performance-improving backbone network, F-Clip is able to significantly outperform all state-of-the-art line detectors on accuracy at a similar or even higher frame rate. In other word, under same inference speed, F-Clip always achieving best accuracy compare with other methods. Source code https://github.com/Delay-Xili/F-Clip.
summary: We present a one-stage Fully Convolutional Line Parsing network (F-Clip) that detects line segments from images. The proposed network is very simple and flexible with variations that gracefully trade off between speed and accuracy for different applications. F-Clip detects line segments in an end-to-end fashion by predicting each line's center position, length, and angle. We further customize the design of convolution kernels of our fully convolutional network to effectively exploit the statistical priors of the distribution of line angles in real image datasets. We conduct extensive experiments and show that our method achieves a significantly better trade-off between efficiency and accuracy, resulting in a real-time line detector at up to 73 FPS on a single GPU. Such inference speed makes our method readily applicable to real-time tasks without compromising any accuracy of previous methods. Moreover, when equipped with a performance-improving backbone network, F-Clip is able to significantly outperform all state-of-the-art line detectors on accuracy at a similar or even higher frame rate. In other word, under same inference speed, F-Clip always achieving best accuracy compare with other methods. Source code https://github.com/Delay-Xili/F-Clip.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种单阶段全卷积线解析网络（F-Clip），可以检测图像中的线段。所提出的网络非常简单且灵活，具有多种变化，可以在不同应用的速度和准确性之间进行优雅的权衡。 F-Clip 通过预测每条线的中心位置、长度和角度来以端到端方式检测线段。我们进一步定制了全卷积网络的卷积核设计，以有效地利用真实图像数据集中线角度分布的统计先验。我们进行了大量的实验，结果表明我们的方法在效率和准确性之间实现了显着更好的权衡，从而在单个 GPU 上实现了高达 73 FPS 的实时线检测器。这样的推理速度使我们的方法很容易适用于实时任务，而不会影响以前方法的任何准确性。此外，当配备性能改进的骨干网络时，F-Clip 能够在相似甚至更高帧速率下的精度方面显着优于所有最先进的线路检测器。换句话说，在相同的推理速度下，与其他方法相比，F-Clip 总是能达到最好的精度。源代码https://github.com/Delay-Xili/F-Clip。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为F-Clip的全卷积线段解析网络，用于从图像中实时检测线段。
AI.motivation: 现有线检测方法在速度与精度之间难以平衡，亟需一种高效且准确的解决方案。
AI.method: 设计了一种端到端的全卷积网络，通过预测线段中心位置、长度和角度实现检测，并优化卷积核以利用真实图像中线段角度的统计先验。
AI.result: 在单GPU上达到73 FPS的实时检测速度，同时精度优于现有方法；配备高性能主干网络时，能在相似或更高帧率下显著提升准确率。
AI.conclusion: F-Clip在保持实时性的同时实现了最优精度，为实时线检测任务提供了高效解决方案。

The proposed network is very simple and flexible with variations that gracefully trade off between speed and accuracy for different applications. F-Clip detects line segments in an end-to-end fashion by predicting each line's center position, length, and angle. We further customize the design of convolution kernels of our fully convolutional network to effectively exploit the statistical priors of the distribution of line angles in real image datasets. We conduct extensive experiments and show that our method achieves a significantly better trade-off between efficiency and accuracy, resulting in a real-time line detector at up to 73 FPS on a single GPU. authors_text: Abhishek Gupta,Justin Yu,Tony Z. Zhao,Vikash Kumar,Aaron Rovinsky,Kelvin Xu,Thomas Devlin,Sergey Levine
title: Reset-Free Reinforcement Learning via Multi-Task Learning: Learning Dexterous Manipulation Behaviors without Human Intervention
title_zh: 通过多任务学习的无重置强化学习：无需人工干预即可学习灵巧的操作行为
comment: Published at ICRA 2021. First four authors contributed equally
comments: Published at ICRA 2021. First four authors contributed equally
abstract: Reinforcement Learning (RL) algorithms can in principle acquire complex robotic skills by learning from large amounts of data in the real world, collected via trial and error. However, most RL algorithms use a carefully engineered setup in order to collect data, requiring human supervision and intervention to provide episodic resets. This is particularly evident in challenging robotics problems, such as dexterous manipulation. To make data collection scalable, such applications require reset-free algorithms that are able to learn autonomously, without explicit instrumentation or human intervention. Most prior work in this area handles single-task learning. However, we might also want robots that can perform large repertoires of skills. At first, this would appear to only make the problem harder. However, the key observation we make in this work is that an appropriately chosen multi-task RL setting actually alleviates the reset-free learning challenge, with minimal additional machinery required. In effect, solving a multi-task problem can directly solve the reset-free problem since different combinations of tasks can serve to perform resets for other tasks. By learning multiple tasks together and appropriately sequencing them, we can effectively learn all of the tasks together reset-free. This type of multi-task learning can effectively scale reset-free learning schemes to much more complex problems, as we demonstrate in our experiments. We propose a simple scheme for multi-task learning that tackles the reset-free learning problem, and show its effectiveness at learning to solve complex dexterous manipulation tasks in both hardware and simulation without any explicit resets. This work shows the ability to learn dexterous manipulation behaviors in the real world with RL without any human intervention.

We propose a simple scheme for multi-task learning that tackles the reset-free learning problem, and show its effectiveness at learning to solve complex dexterous manipulation tasks in both hardware and simulation without any explicit resets. This work shows the ability to learn dexterous manipulation behaviors in the real world with RL without any human intervention.
abstract_zh: 强化学习 (RL) 算法原则上可以通过学习现实世界中通过反复试验收集的大量数据来获得复杂的机器人技能。然而，大多数强化学习算法都使用精心设计的设置来收集数据，需要人工监督和干预来提供间歇性重置。这在具有挑战性的机器人问题中尤其明显，例如灵巧的操作。为了使数据收集可扩展，此类应用程序需要能够自主学习的免重置算法，无需显式仪器或人工干预。该领域的大多数先前工作都处理单任务学习。然而，我们可能还需要能够执行大量技能的机器人。起初，这似乎只会让问题变得更加困难。然而，我们在这项工作中得到的关键观察结果是，适当选择的多任务强化学习设置实际上减轻了免重置学习的挑战，并且只需要最少的额外机制。实际上，解决多任务问题可以直接解决免重置问题，因为任务的不同组合可以用于对其他任务执行重置。通过一起学习多个任务并适当地对它们进行排序，我们可以有效地一起学习所有任务，而无需重置。正如我们在实验中所证明的那样，这种类型的多任务学习可以有效地将免重置学习方案扩展到更复杂的问题。我们提出了一种简单的多任务学习方案，可以解决无重置学习问题，并展示了其在无需任何显式重置的情况下学习解决硬件和模拟中复杂灵巧操作任务的有效性。这项工作展示了利用强化学习在现实世界中学习灵巧操作行为而无需任何人工干预的能力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需重置的强化学习方法，通过多任务学习使机器人能够在没有人工干预的情况下自主学习复杂灵巧操作技能。
AI.motivation: 传统强化学习依赖人工干预进行任务重置，难以实现复杂机器人任务的自主学习。单任务方法无法满足多技能需求，而多任务学习可能通过任务组合实现隐式重置。
AI.method: 设计多任务强化学习框架，通过任务间协同与序列化学习，利用不同任务组合实现隐式重置，无需显式重置机制。
AI.result: 在仿真和真实硬件环境中验证了方法有效性，成功学习复杂灵巧操作任务且无需人工干预或显式重置。
AI.conclusion: 多任务学习可有效解决无重置强化学习问题，为复杂机器人技能的自主学习提供了可扩展方案。

search_text: Reset-Free Reinforcement Learning via Multi-Task Learning: Learning Dexterous Manipulation Behaviors without Human Intervention 通过多任务学习的无重置强化学习：无需人工干预即可学习灵巧的操作行为 Reinforcement Learning (RL) algorithms can in principle acquire complex robotic skills by learning from large amounts of data in the real world, collected via trial and error. However, most RL algorithms use a carefully engineered setup in order to collect data, requiring human supervision and intervention to provide episodic resets. This is particularly evident in challenging robotics problems, such as dexterous manipulation. To make data collection scalable, such applications require reset-free algorithms that are able to learn autonomously, without explicit instrumentation or human intervention. Most prior work in this area handles single-task learning. However, we might also want robots that can perform large repertoires of skills. At first, this would appear to only make the problem harder. However, the key observation we make in this work is that an appropriately chosen multi-task RL setting actually alleviates the reset-free learning challenge, with minimal additional machinery required. In effect, solving a multi-task problem can directly solve the reset-free problem since different combinations of tasks can serve to perform resets for other tasks. By learning multiple tasks together and appropriately sequencing them, we can effectively learn all of the tasks together reset-free. This type of multi-task learning can effectively scale reset-free learning schemes to much more complex problems, as we demonstrate in our experiments. We propose a simple scheme for multi-task learning that tackles the reset-free learning problem, and show its effectiveness at learning to solve complex dexterous manipulation tasks in both hardware and simulation without any explicit resets. This work shows the ability to learn dexterous manipulation behaviors in the real world with RL without any human intervention. 强化学习 (RL) 算法原则上可以通过学习现实世界中通过反复试验收集的大量数据来获得复杂的机器人技能。然而，大多数强化学习算法都使用精心设计的设置来收集数据，需要人工监督和干预来提供间歇性重置。这在具有挑战性的机器人问题中尤其明显，例如灵巧的操作。为了使数据收集可扩展，此类应用程序需要能够自主学习的免重置算法，无需显式仪器或人工干预。该领域的大多数先前工作都处理单任务学习。然而，我们可能还需要能够执行大量技能的机器人。起初，这似乎只会让问题变得更加困难。然而，我们在这项工作中得到的关键观察结果是，适当选择的多任务强化学习设置实际上减轻了免重置学习的挑战，并且只需要最少的额外机制。实际上，解决多任务问题可以直接解决免重置问题，因为任务的不同组合可以用于对其他任务执行重置。通过一起学习多个任务并适当地对它们进行排序，我们可以有效地一起学习所有任务，而无需重置。正如我们在实验中所证明的那样，这种类型的多任务学习可以有效地将免重置学习方案扩展到更复杂的问题。我们提出了一种简单的多任务学习方案，可以解决无重置学习问题，并展示了其在无需任何显式重置的情况下学习解决硬件和模拟中复杂灵巧操作任务的有效性。这项工作展示了利用强化学习在现实世界中学习灵巧操作行为而无需任何人工干预的能力。 本文提出一种无需重置的强化学习方法，通过多任务学习使机器人能够在没有人工干预的情况下自主学习复杂灵巧操作技能。 Abhishek Gupta,Justin Yu,Tony Z.
